lede: A look at March 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ingemar Stenmark and Stéphane Mallarmé, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ozone Disco fire
events.desc: A nightclub fire in Quezon City, Philippines kills 162 people.

events.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
events.desc: Bosnia's Bosniaks and Croats sign the Washington Agreement, ending war between the Croatian Republic of Herzeg-Bosnia and the Republic of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and establishing the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

events.title: Peru
events.desc: Peru: A landslide crashes into Yanawayin Lake, killing 200 people at the mining camp of Chungar.

events.title: Mount Vesuvius
events.desc: Mount Vesuvius in Italy erupts, killing 26 people, causing thousands to flee their homes, and destroying dozens of Allied bombers.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Adolf Hitler and Benito Mussolini meet at the Brenner Pass in the Alps and agree to form an alliance against France and the United Kingdom.

events.title: 1925 Tri-State tornado
events.desc: The 1925 Tri-State tornado hits the Midwestern states of Missouri, Illinois, and Indiana, killing 695 people.

events.title: Mahatma Gandhi
events.desc: In India, Mohandas Gandhi is sentenced to six years in prison for civil disobedience, of which he serves only two.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: During the Battle of Gallipoli, three battleships are sunk during a failed British and French naval attack on the Dardanelles.

events.title: Susenyos I
events.desc: Susenyos is formally crowned Emperor of Ethiopia.

events.title: First Mongol invasion of Poland
events.desc: First Mongol invasion of Poland: Mongols overwhelm Polish armies in Kraków in the Battle of Chmielnik and plunder the city.

births.name: Neville Chamberlain
births.line: English businessman and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

births.name: Rudolf Diesel
births.line: German engineer, invented the Diesel engine

births.name: Grover Cleveland
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 22nd and 24th President of the United States

deaths.text: Chuck Berry, American guitarist, singer and songwriter
Guido Westerwelle, German lawyer and politician, 15th Vice-Chancellor of Germany
J. Sigfrid Edström, Swedish businessman, 4th President of the International Olympic Committee
George I of Greece, King of Greece from 1863 to 1913
Marcellin Berthelot, French chemist and politician, French Minister of Foreign Affairs

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Aimée-Adèle Le Bouteiller
